title: How to save culture in EU research and innovation funding
content:
The next Horizon Europe programme (2028–2034) is being shaped right now, and culture is disappearing from its architecture.

The European Commission’s proposal for the EU long-term budget has no dedicated component for culture or artistic research. The European Parliament’s draft report on Horizon doesn’t fix the proposal: culture gets a nod as a ‘horizontal principle,’ but receives no dedicated home similar to the current Cluster 2. That means less ring-fenced budget, less visibility, and fewer dedicated calls. The draft also proposes to dissolve Horizon’s Society pillar entirely, folding it into a Competitiveness pillar as a fifth policy window.

For this part of the Horizon programme, horizontal means everywhere in theory, nowhere in practice.

That’s why Michael Culture Association, together with the Artistic Research Alliance which is coordinated by ELIA and alongside
